steps.description: Click the "Close" (X) button on the top-right corner of the VS Code welcome dialog to dismiss the sign-in prompt.
Press the "F1" key to open the Command Palette in the Visual Studio Code interface, with the workspace view active.
Type text: 'Welcome: Open Walkthrough' in the Visual Studio Code command palette (invoked via the top search input field).
Press the "Enter" key to select the "Welcome: Open Walkthrough..." command from the command palette in Visual Studio Code.
Type text: 'Build a Declarative Agent' in the dropdown menu labeled 'Select a walkthrough to open' within the Visual Studio Code interface, under the WORKSPACE section.
Press the "Enter" key to select the highlighted search result "Build a Declarative Agent" from the Quick Open bar in the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Set up your environment" radio button under the "Introduction" section of the tutorial interface for Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Check Copilot License" button in the "Set up your environment" section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit welcome screen.
Click the "Sign in" button in the Microsoft 365 notification at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface to access a Microsoft 365 account.
Type ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NO_COPILOT_NAME}}  into the 'Email, phone, or Skype' input field on the Microsoft account sign-in page.
Press the "Enter" key to submit the email address  in the Microsoft login interface, focusing on the "Next" button.
Type the password ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_NO_COPILOT_PASSWORD}}' into the 'Password' field on the Microsoft login page.
Press the "Enter" key to submit the password and proceed from the Microsoft login interface after entering credentials.
Click the 'X' button to close the page
Click the "Check Copilot License" button in the "Set up your environment" section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit welcome interface within Visual Studio Code.
@assertion 'Error: Microsoft 365 account administrator hasn't enabled Copilot access for this account' is visible in the output panel
